Specialized stacked multiple-line chart used in scientific spectroscopy/diffraction contexts (NMR, IR, Raman, UV-vis, MS, XRD). Used to visualize multiple spectra in a single plot, allowing for easy comparison of peak shifts, changes in peak splittings, and signal intensities.

A chromaticity diagram represents the chromaticity of colors, which is defined by two parameters: hue and saturation (or colorfulness). It allows for the visualization of color relationships and is essential in color science for understanding how colors interact and can be reproduced.
Property overlay aligned to the full periodic table layout (rows, groups, blocks), typically showing trends across all or most elements
synonyms/aliases: element coverage map; periodic property chart

short description: Matrix-style visualization linking a subset of elements (e.g. lanthanides) with categorical or binary properties (e.g. precursor availability)
synonyms/aliases: element coverage matrix; element coverage grid
